results.name: Palantir Technologies
keyThemes: PLTR used as IPO resilience benchmark for $SPCX comparison
10x+ post-drawdown recovery narrative driving positive framing
AI sovereignty and enterprise data ownership thesis
Retail investor enthusiasm and Robinhood popularity
Caution around chasing momentum and valuation risk
results.topBullish: Palantir is a good stock gentlemen
results.summary: The dominant narrative across this post sample uses Palantir's post-IPO 80% drawdown followed by a 10x recovery as a bullish template, lending $PLTR an aspirational, resilient brand identity even when posts are primarily promoting $SPCX. A small but notable minority of posts inject caution around momentum-chasing and valuation discipline, while substantive company-specific discussion — including AI sovereignty positioning and enterprise data strategy — remains thin but directionally positive.

results.name: Affirm Holdings

No earnings or competitive positioning commentary
results.topBullish: $afrm Guys, Don't miss the next move in a few   hours 💸 :- https://t.co/QQVJRkIXxC
results.topBearish: @onu_slim BNPL isn't helping broke people. It's teaching people to normalize paying a premium for things they can't yet afford. That's a dangerous financial habit.
results.summary: The vast majority of the 49 posts analyzed contain no meaningful financial signal for Affirm Holdings, as the ticker keyword 'affirm' predominantly captured unrelated social and motivational content. The only actionable posts include a low-credibility pump alert, a broadly negative BNPL sector sentiment comment, and one implicit BNPL critique, yielding an effectively neutral but slightly negative reading with very low analytical confidence.

results.name: iShares Bitcoin ETF
keyThemes: Institutional accumulation and dominant ETF market share
IBIT vs MSTR as preferred BTC exposure vehicle
Bitcoin supply scarcity narrative driving long-term thesis
Bitcoin catching up to gold as macro hedge
Mixed near-term flow signals with some outflow concern
results.topBullish: Bitcoin ETFs saw $1.93B in trading volume. $IBIT led the charge with $1.62B. FBTC followed at $227M, while ARKB traded $44.35M. This level of concentrated activity indicates where institutional attention is heavily focused.
results.topBearish: Recent monthly flows show outflows from spot Bitcoin ETFs and Ethereum ETFs, while leveraged MicroStrategy ETF demand has also faded. The crypto ETF bid is no longer one-way. $BTC $ETH $MSTR $IBIT $FBTC #Crypto #Bitcoin
results.summary: IBIT dominates Bitcoin ETF trading volume with $1.62B of the $1.93B sector total, cementing its position as the institutional vehicle of choice for BTC exposure over alternatives like MSTR. While the long-term bull thesis around fixed BTC supply and Bitcoin-gold convergence remains intact, near-term sentiment is tempered by recent outflow trends and some bearish options positioning, yielding a moderately bullish overall signal.

results.summary: Sentiment around AMZN is modestly bullish, anchored by notable long-term CAGR outperformance data (~27.6% over 20 years) and consistent inclusion in diversified tech portfolios alongside MSFT, NVDA, and GOOGL. The majority of posts are low-signal noise (spam, Prime Video mentions, ticker stuffing), with only one clearly bearish voice citing short-term unpredictability, limiting conviction on near-term directional momentum.

results.topBearish: $KEEL suffers core small-cap infrastructure headwinds: slow project award cadence, thin recurring revenue, and heavy field operation costs that eat into margins. With almost no institutional long-term holders, every price surge only lasts as long as retail social media hype. Once
results.summary: Social media sentiment around $KEEL is cautiously bullish on a net basis, with the majority of posts framing the ~38% drawdown from highs as a margin-call-driven dip in a broader neocloud/AI infrastructure sector selloff rather than a fundamental deterioration. However, signal quality is materially degraded by numerous spam WhatsApp recruitment posts and the absence of any Supply Chain AI or SaaS-specific discussion, suggesting the ticker may be misidentified or the company narrative has pivoted — and that retail hype, not fundamentals, is driving the conversation.

results.name: Iris Energy
keyThemes: AI infrastructure capacity expansion (BC, Texas, SA)
Long-term holder optimism despite price decline
Competitive positioning vs Bitcoin mining peers
results.topBullish: $IREN will profit most, upcoming GW capacity are huge for future contracts! 👍
results.topBearish: Serenity has low in depth $IREN analyst. Today is just another lazy connection. It's highly unlikely that $IREN announces anything in Australia soon because the SA Site is not ready (energized) until 2028. The only sites guided for 2027 1210MW capacity are in BC and Texas.
results.summary: Social media sentiment around $IREN is strongly bullish, dominated by speculation that Iris Energy could secure a high-profile contract with Anthropic tied to a large-scale Australian data center buildout, alongside enthusiasm about its multi-gigawatt capacity pipeline in BC and Texas as a structural AI infrastructure beneficiary. A notable counterpoint flags that the South Australia site is not energized until 2028, tempering near-term Anthropic deal expectations, while some holders acknowledge recent price weakness and eagerness for a positive catalyst.

results.topBullish:

The EU should stop pretending basic driver monitoring and lane-assist systems are the future of road safety

Make it easier for manufacturers to adopt Tesla FSD instead of pushing box-checking around weaker driver-assist systems with unknown real-world effectiveness
results.topBearish:
$TSLA Despite beating delivery number expectations, the stock is down breaking below the 400 whole number. This is likely a case of "buy the rumor, sell the news." This week I will be looking for a continuation move down if this stays below 400.

Short idea below 390
results.summary: Sentiment on TSLA is broadly neutral with modest bullish undercurrents centered on FSD adoption momentum in Europe and potential Japan market entry, offset by near-term bearish price action as the stock trades below key moving averages ($399 20-day, $406 50-day) following a classic buy-the-rumor sell-the-news delivery beat. Reputational noise around Elon Musk and a predominance of spam/off-topic posts dilute signal quality, keeping conviction low despite the marginally positive fundamental storyline.

results.name: QXO Inc
results.topBullish:
$QXO shareholders have overwhelmingly approved the acquisition of $BLD TopBuild, with approximately 99% of votes cast in favour.

The deal creates one of the largest building products distribution platforms in North America. https://t.co/i86mGnmBF5
results.summary: Sentiment around QXO Inc is cautiously bullish, anchored by the landmark near-unanimous shareholder approval of the TopBuild acquisition, which positions the company as one of North America's largest building products distributors and validates the M&A rollup thesis. Retail interest is growing with new position initiations appearing in diversified Roth portfolios, though a notable portion of social activity consists of promotional trading-group spam and vague references, limiting signal quality.
